---
title: "Imad Dabbura"
subtitle: "AI Scientist"
description: "AI Scientist building ML systems from first principles."
image: images/profile-pic-web.jpg
about:
  template: trestles
  image-width: 14em
  image-shape: rounded
  id: hero-heading
  links:
    - icon: twitter
      text: Twitter
      href: https://twitter.com/imadphd
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/imaddabbura/
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/imaddabbura
    - icon: medium
      text: Medium
      href: https://medium.com/@ImadPhd
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:imad.dabbura@hotmail.com
page-layout: full
css: assets/about.css
---

::: {#hero-heading}
Welcome to your backstage pass for mastering AI and Machine Learning — not through surface-level tutorials, but through first-principles thinking, mathematical derivations, and building things from scratch.

I'm an AI Scientist with a decade of experience across smart devices, healthcare, and enterprise AI. The best way I've found to truly understand something is to derive the math — whether it's backpropagation, probability theory, or information-theoretic bounds — write the code, and dig into every layer. That's what this site is about.

### What You'll Find Here

- **From-Scratch Builds** — GPT-2, LSTMs, transformers, a PyTorch-like framework, and more — every line annotated with the reasoning behind it.
- **Mathematical Foundations** — Deep dives into probability, information theory, optimization, and the statistical machinery that underpins modern ML.
- **Battle-Tested Lessons** — What actually works when you move from notebook to production, what breaks along the way, and how to skip the growing pains.
- **Paper Breakdowns** — Structured summaries of foundational and influential papers, distilled into what matters and why.
- **Open-Source Tools** — Libraries and frameworks built to accelerate ML experimentation.

### Get in Touch

I'm always looking to improve and tailor content to what's most useful. If you have a correction, a question, or a suggestion for what to write about next — reach out by email or on Twitter.
:::
